description: §52.8 SSR first-paint + §14.8.9 redaction — the RUNTIME half of item 1/3 (ss60-parked, adapter-gated). The E-ADAPTER server-eval SSR path invokes the REAL _scrml_ssr_compose_handler: a server-authority <each> over a protect= table pre-renders its rows INTO the first-paint HTML (keyed data-scrml-key, view-source-visible before client JS), and those rows feed on the §14.8.9-redacted seed — the protected column (passwordHash / its value) is ABSENT from both the rendered rows AND the window.__scrml_ssr_state seed. The client then hydrates against that first paint (accounts = the redacted rows). Closes the runtime half the D2 browser test proved only with a hand-built protectAnalysis; here the whole compile pipeline derives it from source.
rationale: §52.8 (SPEC.md §52 line ~30252): 'a view-source of the first paint already contains the rows ... It feeds on the §14.8.9-redacted rows (a protected column is absent — no first-paint confidentiality leak).' auth="none" makes the cell public (so I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED does not fire and the cell is NOT auto-omitted from the SSR seed — the §52.15.5 auto-make-safe omission is gated on auth-scoping), which is orthogonal to the column-redaction floor asserted here (§52 line ~30500 — the three axes STACK).
